observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Inventory Committee, the MGSO Designate and the Municipal Accountant to properly plan and prepare the PIP to ensure an orderly conduct of the one-time cleansing of PPE following the guidelines prescribed in COA Circular No. 2020-006 to account for all PPE, reconcile the property accounting records and resolve unreconciled discrepancies between the total PPE per books and per property records;
b. Inventory Committee:
1. Prepare a separate IIRUP and reconstitute Disposal Committee to facilitate disposal of these properties; and
2. Properly prepare the RPCPPE using the prescribed format in Appendix 49 and group PPEs by type/classification/account in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs.
c. MGSO Designate and/or the Municipal Accountant to:
1. Attend to the manpower needs and demands in the assignment of personnel in their respective Offices to perform such duties in relation to the PPE owned and controlled by the Municipality; and
2. Consider acquiring a computerized accounting system with the capability to generate audit-trail reports/valuable information and financial statements such as the PPE Ledger Cards, depreciation schedule and details in General Ledger, and to produce reports such as the Property Acknowledgement Receipt and Property Cards, among others, for easy identification and reconciliation of records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer to exert effort to trace the details of the noted difference amounting to P117,226,335.93 between their records and effect the necessary adjustments, if warranted;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to immediately prepare, update, and submit the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for all bank accounts for CY 2024 and ensure continuous monthly submission thereafter; and henceforth
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to conduct periodic reconciliation and review of cash balances between the Cashbook and bank statements to immediately detect and correct discrepancies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer to discontinue the practice of granting additional or new cash advances to AOs with outstanding cash advances unless their previous advances have been fully liquidated;
b. Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer to propose practical and effective measures to address delays in liquidation, such as, if necessary, designating additional Accountable Officers to manage the Municipal’s cash advances, sending demand letters to concerned officers and employees who have not liquidated their cash advances within the prescribed period, with a reminder that their salaries may be withheld if they fail to liquidate after the demand; and henceforth
c. Strict enforcement of compliance with the granting and utilization of all cash advances in accordance with PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Prioritize the preparation and maintenance of Subsidiary Ledgers for both the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts and ensure that they accurately reflect all transactions;
b. Reconcile the balances of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts, ensuring that each entry in the Due from account corresponds to an entry in the Due to account and vice versa; and
c. Strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 2015-009 to ensure that the balances of the Due from and Due to Other Funds accounts are in agreement and in compliance with established accounting standards.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Retrieve and consolidate all documents and records pertaining to the fund transfers received from various National Government Agencies (NGAs), including fund utilization reports, disbursement vouchers, and official communications; and
b. Prepare and maintain Subsidiary Ledgers for the Due to NGAs account, ensuring that each entry is supported by sufficient documentation and includes all necessary details such as source agency, date and amount of fund receipt, and specific disbursements made to facilitate proper monitoring, verification, and validation of the account, and ensure its accurate and fair presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to disclose the required financial information in the Notes to the Financial Statements in the ensuing year, ensuring compliance with Paragraph 1 of IPSAS 2 and Paragraphs 127 and 129 of IPSAS 1 to assist users in understanding the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as the Chairman of the Local School Board, to instruct the following:
a. The Municipal Accountant to prepare the Quarterly and Annual SEF Budget Accountability Forms on SEF Utilization for CY 2024, and ensure continuous compliance in the succeeding years;
b. The Local School Board to:
1. Submit quarterly and annual reports using the SEF Budget Accountability Form No. 1 (Annex B) on SEF Utilization to the DepEd CO, through appropriate channels, copy furnished the Sangguniang Bayan and Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG; and
2. Post the utilization report of the SEF Budget in three conspicuous locations for transparency and accountability in compliance with the Full Disclosure Policy of the DILG.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant, Acting Municipal Treasurer and HRMO to conduct a thorough reconciliation of the discrepancies among the payrolls, the General Ledger, and the balances presented in the Statement of Financial Position to identify and correct any recording errors or omissions related to remittances made to the GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth;
b. Municipal Accountant and the HRMO to validate the unremitted contributions under the Due to PhilHealth accounts to establish accurate balances and immediately remit the amounts payable; and henceforth
c. Acting Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the GSIS, PAG-IBIG and PhilHealth regulations on the remittance of withheld contributions to avoid the risk of potential penalties and legal consequences.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer to:
a. Strictly refrain from charging expenditures against the 20% DF that are not aligned with the allowable items prescribed under Section 3.2.5 of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1, and which do not directly contribute to the attainment of the Municipality’s socio-economic development and environmental management goals; and
b. Ensure the exclusion of non-allowable expenditures in the preparation of the ensuing annual budget, and that only projects and activities with tangible, measurable, and development-focused outcomes be proposed under the 20% DF, in compliance with existing guidelines and to promote fiscal discipline and fund optimization.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to coordinate with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to establish a complete GAD Database containing gender statistics and sex-disaggregated data and identify gender issues based on the gender analysis, in accordance with Section 36 (c) of RA No. 9710 and Item 4.1 B of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the Management.
observations.reason: The Management is in the process of completing the requirements needed in the adoption of One-Time Cleansing for PPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC- Municipal Accountant to draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to adjust the reconciling items in the books of accounts and henceforth, strictly comply with Section 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011 and Section 7.2.1 of the RRSA prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-006.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the Management.
observations.reason: Delays in verification occurred due to manual processing and lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) require all personnel with outstanding cash advances to immediately settle their unliquidated cash advances;
b) instruct the OIC- Municipal Accountant that subsequent grant of cash advance shall be supported with certification that their previous cash advances were already liquidated and or proper accounting thereof is made; and
c) impose the withholding of salary of accountable officers who failed to effect the prompt liquidation of their cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Not all personnel with outstanding cash advances were able to settle their unliquidated cash advances. The OIC- Municipal Accountant explained that it is very vital for the Municipality to not hamper the grant of additional cash advance especially if it is for social services.
observations.reason: Some accountable officers are no longer connected to the agency.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC- Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to:
a) disclose the required financial information in the Notes to Financial Statements in the ensuing year to assist the users in understanding the financial statements;
b) conduct inventory of the Local Road Network and prepare and submit the RLRN and RPCLRN yearly; and
c) henceforth, strictly comply with the guidelines and provisions in COA Circular No. 2015-008.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the Management.
observations.reason: The RLRN and RPCLRN which are the basis for disclosure was not submitted to the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC- Municipal Accountant to:
a) record/set-up the Special Education Tax Receivables and Deferred Special Education Tax for CY 2022, furnishing the Office of the Auditor with a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) taking into account the adjustments made; and
b) henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of Section 20 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units, Volume I and Sections 233 and 235 of the Local Government Code of 1991.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the Management.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC- Municipal Accountant to:
a) prepare and submit the Reconciliation of Net Cash from Operating Activities to Surplus/Deficit;
b) disclose the required financial information in the Notes to Financial Statements in the ensuing year to assist the users in understanding the financial statements; and
c) henceforth, strictly comply with Paragraph 1 of IPSAS 2 and Paragraphs 127 and 129 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: The OIC- Municipal Accountant tried to prepare the Reconciliation of Net Cash from Operating Activities to Surplus/Deficit but she was not able to reconcile, hence reconciliation was not presented in the NTFS. Moreover, other information required to support significant accounts in the FS were not provided because NTFS was manually prepared.
observations.reason: Delays in reconciliation occurred due to manual processing of transactions and lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Assessor to:
a) see to it that revision of real property assessments be made every three years to update the fair market value of taxable properties and derive additional income therefrom;
b) give ample time to complete the revision process for the documents indorsed by BLGF to assure its effectivity on CY 2024; and
c) henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of Section 219 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the Management.
observations.reason: Depends upon the issuance of Sangguniang Panlalawigan Resolution on the revision of real property tax assessments.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MPDC and the OIC-Municipal Engineer to:
a) address the cause/s for the delay and/or non-implementation of the projects;
b) maximize the utilization of the 20 percent Development Fund to help the budget achieve desirable socio-economic development outcomes; and
c) henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of Section 5 of the Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG) and Department of Budget and Management (DBM) Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the Management. Some provisions of the JMC were already complied except for Section 3.2.3 of the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 2020-1.
observations.reason: Some PPAs included in the appropriation were not allowable to be charged under 20 percent development fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a) Direct the OIC- Municipal Treasurer to prepare RPCPPE for each year
for submission on or before January 31 of the following year;

b) Instruct the OIC- Municipal Accountant and the OIC- Municipal
Treasurer to maintain and update PPELC and PC, respectively, to
facilitate reconciliation of the PPE accounts;

c) Adopt the guidelines and procedures for one-time cleansing of PPE
account balances as provided by COA Circular No. 2020-006; and

d) Henceforth, strictly observe compliance with pertinent provisions of
MNGAS for LGUs and COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC- Municipal
Treasurer to:

a) update and balance daily the transactions recorded in the cashbooks;

b) at the end of the month, rule and foot the cashbooks and accomplish
the monthly certification required; and

c) henceforth, strictly comply with Sections 26 and 27 of MNGAS for
LGUs, Volume II.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the
OIC- Municipal Accountant to draw Journal Entry Voucher to adjust the
reconciling items in the books of accounts and henceforth, strictly comply
with Section 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011 and Section 7.2.1 of the RRSA,
prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-006.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a) require all personnel with outstanding cash advances to immediately
settle their unliquidated cash advances;

b) require the OIC- Municipal Accountant that subsequent grant of cash
advance shall be supported with certification that their previous cash
advances were already liquidated and or proper accounting thereof is made;

c) instruct the OIC- Municipal Accountant to record the cash advances of
officers and employees and that of the disbursing officer in
accordance with the Local Government Chart of Accounts; and

d) reconcile the figures appearing in the financial statements and the
schedule submitted to the Office of the Auditor for proper presentation
of the accounts in the financial statements.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-
Municipal Accountant and OIC- Municipal Treasurer to:

a) disclose the required financial information in the NTFS in the ensuing
year to assist the users in understanding the financial statements;

b) conduct inventory of the Local Road Network and prepare and submit
the RLRN and RPCLRN yearly; and

c) henceforth, strictly comply with the prescribed format provided in COA
Circular No. 2015-008.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayort instruct the
OIC- Municipal Accountant to:

a) reconcile the difference in the reciprocal accounts Due from/Due to
make sure that the entries in Due from Other Funds accounts have
corresponding entries in the Due to Other Funds account and vice versa;

b) continue the reconciliation of Due from Other Funds and Due to Other
Funds accounts to totally eliminate the difference; and

c) strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2015-009 and IPSAS 1 to ensure
that the balances of both accounts are in agreement.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC- Municipal
Accountant to:

a. retrieve all documents and other records pertaining to the fund transfer
received by the Municipality from different NGAs; and

b. prepare and maintain SLs containing all the necessary details such as
receipts and disbursements chargeable against the Due to NGAs
accounts for immediate verification and validation of the accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a) OIC- Municipal Accountant to draw Journal Entry Voucher to adjust the
amount of staled checks in the books of accounts;

b) OIC- Municipal Treasurer to monitor the checks that were not yet
encashed in the depository bank and notify the payees of the existence of
the checks to avoid its being stale; and

c) strictly comply with Section 59, Chapter 3 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor provide cut-off dates for the early
submission of disbursement vouchers and checks specifically for cash
advances to stop the incorrect practice.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant refrain from recording
cash advances as part of Accounts Payable for proper presentation of the
accounts in the financial statements. We further recommend that upon
liquidation of the cash advances, credit the account Prior Period
Adjustments instead of the actual expenses.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-
Municipal Accountant to:

a) prepare and submit the Reconciliation of Net Cash from Operating
Activities to Surplus/Deficit;

b) disclose the required financial information in the NTFS in the ensuing
year to assist the users in understanding the FS; and

c) henceforth, strictly comply with Paragraph 1 of IPSAS 2 and Paragraphs
127 and 129 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

• direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to process the application for
insurance with the GSIS of all insurable assets and properties of the
Municipality following the guidelines prescribed under COA Circular No.
2018-002;
• instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to include in the Municipality’s
annual budget the appropriation for the payment of insurance premiums
of all insurable assets and properties of the Municipality;
• direct the OIC- Municipal Accountant to provide necessary assistance to
the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all insurable assets and
properties of the Municipality will be covered with insurance in
compliance with RA No. 656 and COA Circular No. 2018-002; and
• Prepare and issue Deed of Transfer or Deed of Donation to the 19 motor
vehicles issued to barangays together with the OR/CR. Then drop them
from the books of the Municipality and require the barangays to record
them in their respective books of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Management consider shifting to APS
for processing and payment of salaries and wages of personnel under Job
Order to ensure better control over handling of cash, administrative
efficiency and mitigate the risk of loss, misapporopriation, and other risks
associated with manual handling of cash.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget
Officer to refrain from charging the above-cited expenses that do not
contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and
environmental management outcomes of the Municipality.

We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) exclude
expenditures which are not allowed to be charged against the 20 percent
development fund in the next budget preparation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDRRMO to:

• adequately plan and monitor the PPAs listed in the LDRRMIP for
effective utilization of LDRRMF; and
• henceforth strictly comply with the provisions of Section 21 of RA no.
10121 and the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GAD Focal Person
to:

• establish the GAD database with adequate and systematically
gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for
gender analysis, planning, programming, and policy formulation; and

• undertake gender analysis based on GAD database to identify and
confirm existing gender issues and ensure that the programs,
projects, and activities are responsive to the said issues.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC- Municipal
Accountant to review the disbursement vouchers prepared for the remittance
of taxes which shall cover the total amount of taxes withheld from source to
avoid penalties and over remittance of taxes.

Henceforth, strictly observe compliance with pertinent provisions of
Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 on withholding and remittance of taxes and
PPSAS I for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC- Municipal
Accountant to see to it that disbursement vouchers prepared for the
remittance of Due to GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG accounts covered the
total amount of deductions from source; and

Strictly observe compliance with pertinent laws, rules and regulations on
mandatory deductions from salaries of government employees and
remittance to the concerned agencies.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC- Municipal Accountant to:
a) reconcile the difference in the reciprocal accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds and make the necessary adjustments to ensure that the balances of both accounts are in agreement; and
b) ensure that the entries in Due from Other Funds accounts have corresponding entries in the Due to Other Funds account and vice versa.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the Management. Reconciliation is on going and the difference was reduced to P7,151,018.02 in CY 2023 from P8,209,767.21 in CY 2022.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of the accounts is in the process.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) correct the erroneous entry of PPE and Advances to Contractors; and
b) see to it that Advances to Contractors account is credited with an amount equal to the percentage of the total contract price used for the advance payment every progress billing; and
c) strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-009 and Annex E of R-IRR of Republic Act No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) was already prepared. The Accounting Office reviewed the amount credited to Advances to Contractors.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to correct the erroneous entries;
b) obtain a copy of GPPA Status Report on Programs, Projects and Activities (GPPAs) from Engineering Office, for reconciliation and basis for recording in the books of accounts; and
c) henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of Section 50 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I and IPSAS No. 17 in recording the cost of ongoing projects.
observations.managementAction: JEV was already drawn to correct the erroneous entries; GPPAs were already furnished to Accounting Office. The Municipality complied with the provisions of Section 50 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I and IPSAS No. 17.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the HRMO to:
a) prepare the contract of services, MOA and job order between the Municipality and the individual;
b) submit the contract of services, MOA and job order to the CSCRO for review; and
c) strictly adhere with the provisions of (CSC) Resolution No. 02-0790 regarding policy guidelines for contract of services, MOA and job order.
observations.managementAction: The contract of services, MOA and job order between the Municipality of Cavinti and the individual was already prepared. The submission of Job Orders to CSCRO for review was repealed by CSC Resolution No. 06-2254 circularized by CSC Memorandum Circular (MC) No. 01, series of 2007.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GAD Focal Point Person to:
a) ensure that all the PPAs included in the GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) will be implemented during the year so that the envisioned GAD objectives and results will be fully achieved; and
b) henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of Philippine Commission on Women (PCW), DILG, DBM and National Economic and Development Authority (NEDA) JMC No. 2013-01, as amended.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person informed that they already coordinated with the barangays for the submission of LCCAP filled-up survey form as well as master list per sector such as women, youth, senior citizens and persons with disability for the establishment and maintenance of LGU GAD Database.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that the Management direct the General Services Officer to:

a) coordinate with the Accounting Office to reconcile their property records and to ascertain the cause of the total discrepancy;

b) submit the itemized Inventory of Infrastructure Assets, Land and Buildings and ensure that all the relevant information such as Fund Name, Property Number and Year Acquired are disclosed in the RPCPPE; and

c) henceforth, strictly observe compliance with Section 124 0f the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC- Municipal Accountant to draw Journal Entry Voucher to adjust the reconciling items in the books of accounts and henceforth, strictly comply with Section 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011 and Section 7.2.1 of the RRSA prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-006.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor:

a) require the OIC- Municipal Accountant to draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to adjust the amount of stale checks in the books of accounts;

b) require the Municipal Treasurer to monitor the checks that were not yet encashed in the depository bank and notify the payees of the existence of the checks to avoid its being stale; and

c) henceforth, strictly comply with Section 59, Chapter 3 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Management instruct the Acting Municipal Accountant to:

a) make sure that the entries in Due from Other Funds accounts have corresponding entries in the Due to Other Funds account and vice versa; and

b) reconcile the difference in the reciprocal accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds and make the necessary adjustments to ensure that the balances of both accounts are in agreement.

We reiterated our recommendations that that the Municipal Mayor direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to:

a) make an extensive review and evaluation of the accounting records to determine the causes of the abnormal balances of liabilities;

b) prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balances; and

c) henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-009 and Section 03(s) of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for LGUs, Volume I.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor instruct:

a) the Acting Municipal Accountant to set-up RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year based on duly certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year provided by the Municipal Treasurer;

b) the Municipal Treasurer to start the preparation of the duly certified list of taxpayers and the total amount due and collectible at the beginning of the year and; and

c) henceforth, strictly comply with Section 20 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor request the Acting Municipal Accountant to include in the Notes to Financial Statements the Reconciliation of Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities to Surplus/Deficit and henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of IPSAS 2.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to:

a) disclose the required financial information in the Notes to Financial Statements in the ensuing year to assist the users in understanding the financial statements;

b) provide particulars in recording in the subsidiary ledgers to identify the details of the transactions; and

c) henceforth, strictly comply with Paragraph 1 of IPSAS 2 and Paragraphs 127 and 129 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Acting Municipal Accountant to:

a) prepare and submit the BRS within the reglementary period;

b) prepare the necessary adjusting entries for any reconciling items identified to establish the year-end balances of the cash accounts; and

c) henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of Items 3.2 to 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011 and Section 4.1 of COA Circular No. 2010-001.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator:

a) Evaluate the necessity of the unimplemented projects and reprogram the same to more important development projects that will cater to the present needs of the constituents;

b) Ensure that all projects funded out of the 20 percent DF will be implemented during the year in order to attain the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes; and

c) Henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of Section 5 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books of accounts all Infrastructure Assets.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Department had recognized in the books of accounts all Infrastructure Assets.

observations.recommendation:
a. Conduct a complete annual physical count of PPE by type and submit the duly approved Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE),
contrary to the provisions of Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Section C.3, Chapter V of the Manual on Property Custodianship.
b. Report the inventory of PPE by type or account name;
c. Coordinate with the Acting Municipal Accountant for the proper classification of PPEs in order that the PPEs will be reported appropriately in the RPCPPE; and
d. Delete from PPE items with acquisition cost of less than ₱10,000.00 and record them under Inventory Held for Consumption for proper presentation in the financial statements.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Acting Municipal Accountant to provide the necessary assistance to the Municipal Treasurer with regard the proper classification of PPEs for a consistent and comparable reporting. Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Acting Municipal Accountant regularly reconcile their property records to readily determine the cause of any discrepancies and thereafter make the necessary adjustments.
observations.managementAction:
a. Physical count was conducted. A memo was issued to the concerned department/offices to coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer Office.
b. The revision of RPCPPE is on-going.
c. The Municipal Treasurer coordinated with the Acting Municipal Accountant for the proper classification of PPEs.
d. Preparation of Inventory Held for Consumption is on-going.
The Acting Municipal Accountant provided the necessary assistance to the Municipal Treasurer with regard the proper classification of PPEs for a consistent and comparable reporting.
The Municipal Treasurer and Acting Municipal Accountant started the reconciliation of their property records to readily determine the cause of any discrepancies and thereafter make the necessary adjustments.
observations.reason:
Physical count of equipment is still on-going.
Voluminous transaction in the Treasurer’s Office.
Due to voluminous transactions, reconciliation is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to draw Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) to record the reconciling items identified in the Bank Reconciliation Statements for proper presentation of the Cash in Bank-LCCA in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Some of the reconciling items were already adjusted.
observations.reason: The Acting Municipal Accountant had started the process of reconciling the accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor:
(a) require the Acting Municipal Accountant to draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to adjust the amount of stale checks in the books of accounts;
(b) require the Municipal Treasurer to monitor the checks that were not yet encashed in the depository bank and notify the payees of the existence of the checks to avoid its being stale; and
(c) henceforth, strictly comply with Section 59, Chapter 3 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor will issue a memorandum to temporarily assign a personnel to review and update the Bank Reconciliation Statements; after which the Acting Municipal Accountant will draw JEV to adjust the amount of stale checks in the books of accounts.
observations.reason: Current issuance of checks following the procedures stated under Section 59, Chapter 3 of NGAS Manual, Volume I, for LGUs. Reiterated in Observation No. 3, Page No. 35, Part II, of this report.

observations.recommendation:
a. Acting Municipal Accountant to take up the uncollected receivables by adjusted the JEV debiting Real Property Tax Receivables and crediting Deferred Real Property Tax Income/Deferred Special Education Tax Income at the beginning of the year; and
b. Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Acting Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the name of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year as basis in drawing a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to set-up the Real Property Tax Receivable/Special Education Tax Receivable and Deferred Real Property Tax Income/Deferred Special Education Tax Income at the beginning of the year; and
c. Municipal Treasurer to undertake effective, efficient and aggressive tax collection effort and develop strategies to improve their tax collection.
observations.managementAction:
The Acting Municipal Accountant had not yet recorded/adjusted the JEV preparation due to the volume of transactions.
The Municipal Treasurer is in the process of preparing the duly certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible during the year. The Municipal Treasurer had prepared and submitted the necessary documents to COA for the audit. The Municipal Treasurer had prepared and developed strategies to improve their tax collection.
observations.reason:
The preparation of list of taxpayers was not completed due to the volume of taxpayers.
Reiterated in Observation No. 6, Page No. 39, Part II, of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Management instruct the Acting Municipal Accountant to:
a) make sure that the entries in Due from Other Funds accounts have corresponding entries in the Due to Other Funds account and vice versa; and
b) reconcile the difference in the reciprocal accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds and make the necessary adjustments to ensure that the balances of both accounts are in agreement.
observations.managementAction: The Acting Municipal Accountant is currently conducting the reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts and will make the necessary adjustments upon completion.
observations.reason:
Lack of manpower to focus on the reconciliation of the accounts.
Reiterated in Observation No. 4, Page No. 36, Part II, of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to:
a) make an extensive review and evaluation of the accounting records to determine the causes of the abnormal balances of liabilities;
b) prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balances; and

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Acting Municipal
Accountant to recognize in the books of accounts all the Public Infrastructures
that were in the registries and directly record in the books of accounts all
subsequent Infrastructure Assets that will be completed.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
• conduct a complete annual physical count of PPE by type and submit the
  duly approved Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) using the
  format prescribed under Annex 49 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II;
• report the inventory of PPE by type or account name;
• coordinate with the Acting Municipal Accountant for the proper
  classification of PPEs in order that PPEs will be appropriately reported in the
  RPCPPE; and
• delete from PPE items the procured supplies and materials and record them
  under Inventory Held for Consumption for proper presentation ot the
  account in the financial statements.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Acting Municipal
Accountant to provide the necessary assistance to the Municipal Treasurer with
regard the proper classification of PPEs for a consistent and comparable
reporting.

Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Acting Municipal
Accountant regularly reconcile their property records to readily determine the
cause of any discrepancies and thereafter make the necessary adjustments.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
draw Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) to record the reconciling items identified in
the Bank Reconciliation Statements for proper presentation of the Cash in Bank-
LCCA in the financial statements.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Acting
Municipal Accountant to draw Journal Entry Vouchers to adjust the amount of
staled checks in the books of accounts following the procedures stated under
Section 59, Chapter 3 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I. We also recommended
the monitoring of released checks that were not yet encashed in the depository
bank and notify the payees of the existence of the checks to avoid its being stale.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a) Acting Municipal Accountant to take up the uncollected receivables by
   debiting Real Property Tax Receivables/Special Education Tax Receivables
   and crediting Deferred Real Property Tax Income/Deferred Special
   Education Tax income in the books of General Fund and Special Education
   Fund, respectively;
b) Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Acting Municipal Accountant a duly
   certified list showing the name of the taxpayers and the amount due and
   collectible for the year as basis in setting-up the Real Property Tax
   Receivable/Special Education Tax Receivable and Deferred Real Property
   Tax Income/Deferred Special Education Tax Income at the beginning of the
   year; and
c) Municipal Treasurer to undertake effective, efficient and aggressive tax
   collection effort and develop strategies to improve their tax collection
   efficiency.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the
Municipal Accountant to reconcile the difference in the reciprocal accounts Due
from Other Funds and Due To Other Funds and make the necessary adjustments
to ensure that the balances of both accounts are in agreement.

We recommended that that the Municipal Mayor direct the Acting Municipal
Accountant to make an extensive review and evaluation of the accounting
records to determine the causes of the abnormal balances of liabilities and
prepare the necessary adjustments.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a) Acting Municipal Accountant to record purchases of goods/supplies and
   materials as inventory and to recognize expenses upon issuance based on
   the SSMI;
b) Acting Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer
   (GSO) to maintain Updated Subsidiary Ledger Cards and Updated Stock
   Cards to account for the receipt and disposition of supplies and materials;
c) Municipal GSO or the Department Heads to conduct and submit inventory of
   unused supplies and materials at year-end as basis for the recording of
   inventory accounts in the financial statements.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Acting
Municipal Accountant to disclose the required financial information in the Notes to
Financial Statements in the ensuing year to assist the users in understanding the
financial statements, and to provide them with additional information value on the
accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. MGSO to submit to the Office of the Auditor a request for disposal of all
   unserviceable motor vehicles together with the Inventory and Inspection
   Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP), Appraisal Report, and other
   required documents for appropriate action once the appraisal has been
   conducted and the documents required are complete.
b. Appraisal and Disposal Committee to immediately undertake an appraisal of
   these unserviceable property and prepare a corresponding report thereon; and
c. Municipal Treasurer to initiate the process of disposing of unserviceable
   property in accordance with the rules and procedures provided in COA
   Circular No. 92-386 and drop these unserviceable properties in the books
   based on the IIRUP.

We commended the Management of the Municipal Government of Cavinti for the
efficient and prompt implementation of the identified programs, projects and
activities towards the achievement of the BGCM’s objective.

However, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a) Acting Municipal Accountant to:
• submit immediately the lacking documents for the mentioned disbursements
  for further verification by the Audit Team;
• refrain from recognizing as outright expense those items classified as
  inventory, instead follow the recording and reporting procedures provided
  under Section 114 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
b) Municipal Treasurer to maintain the required reports for proper monitoring
  and reporting of the receipts and issuances of inventories and coordinate
  with the Acting Municipal Accountant for the proper amount of inventory that
  will be recognized as expense for a period;
c) Acting Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer or any
  appropriate Municipal official to coordinate with the Acting Municipal
  Accountant for the submission of the documents to substantiate the relief
  operations made by the Municipality and for the Audit Team to determine
  whether the procured relief goods were completely distributed to the
  intended beneficiaries;
d) Municipal Budget Officer or any appropriate Municipal official to submit a
  written justification explaining the rationale for the charging of the
  inappropriate disbursements totalling P66,680.00 against the BGCM fund; and
e) Concerned officials to comply with the posting requirement for information
  and transparency.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning
Development Council Officer to:
a) Evaluate the necessity of the unimplemented projects, especially those in

   that will cater to the present needs of the constituents;
b) Ensure that all projects funded out of the 20 percent DF will be implemented
   during the year in order to attain the desired socio-economic development
   and environmental outcomes.

Acting Municipal Accountant to appropriate expenditures specifically related to
the objectives of 20 percent Development Fund in the next budget and refrain
from charging expenses that do not contribute to the attainment of desirable
socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes of the
Municipality.

Reduction and Management Council to fully utilize the current appropriation for
LDRRMF.

Trust Fund at the end of each year.

and Management Officer submit a written justification explaining the rationale for
the charging of the P289,000.00 financial assistance against the LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point Person to
ensure that the PPAs included in the GAD Plan and Budget will be immediately
implemented in order that the envisioned GAD objectives and results will be fully

We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendations that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
Acting Municipal Accountant:
to record purchases of supplies as debit to appropriate asset account using the perpetual inventory method regardless of whether or not they were consumed within the accounting period;
to maintain Subsidiary Ledger Cards for each supplies and materials; and
to record the issuances based on the Report of  Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI).
Supply Officer:
to use Requisition Issue Slip (RIS) for the issuance of supplies and maintain Stock Cards;
to prepare and submit monthly the RSMI to the Acting Municipal Accountant to serve as basis in recording supply issuances; and
prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to report the physical count of supplies not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain complete subsidiary records for each special account and prepare trial balance and financial statements for each special account at the end of the each year, in compliance with Section 110 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to strictly implement the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances to prevent the accumulation of unliquidated cash advances and enforce withholding of salaries of Accountable Officers who fail to effect prompt liquidation of their cash advances.
We also recommended that the concerned Accountable Officers immediately liquidate their outstanding cash advances.
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Acting Municipal Accountant settle obligations by drawing checks payable to suppliers.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the LCPC to fully utilize the appropriated amount for LCPC for PPAs authorized under RA No. 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor through the Municipal Development Council and in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator formulate programs, projects and activities under the 20% Development Fund in accordance with DILG-DBM JMC No. 2011-1;  ensure the optimum utilization of the fund and fully implement the planned projects, programs and activities so that the desired services and benefits are delivered to the constituents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to undertake effective, efficient and aggressive tax collection effort and develop strategies to improve their tax collection efficiency. Aside from sending Notice of Delinquency, update the Provincial Treasurer on the matter of legal remedies for application of administrative and judicial actions to enforce collections of delinquent real property tax.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipality account all the remaining unutilized income from Share from National Wealth and proportionately allocate the 80% of its share derived from national wealth to lower the cost of electricity in the community and the remaining 20% to finance development and livelihood projects.
We also recommended for the submission of explanation on the use of the fund for expenses not related to lower the cost of electricity consumption in the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Government thru its LGU GFPS fully utilize the GAD Budget and implement the GAD planned activities to promote gender-responsive governance, protecting and fulfilling women’s human rights, and promoting women’s empowerment.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipality establish the GAD Database to strengthen the planning and budgeting processes for GAD and ensure that planned PPAs address identified gender issues, GAD objectives, performance indicators and targets are well-formulated, to facilitate the monitoring and evaluation of the attainment of GAD objectives.

We recommended that the Municipal Government thru its LDRRMC fully utilize the Mitigation Fund/preparedness budget for the implementation of LDRRMF PPAs to better prepare and lessen the impact of deadly and costly natural and man-made hazards and disasters.
We further recommended that the Municipality implement projects/programs and utilize the LDRRMF solely for disaster prevention, mitigation and preparedness in compliance with RA No. 10121.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to transfer the unexpended balance of the QRF and MF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund of the Trust Fund books.
